summary: Clarified Metal render evidence wording by replacing the ambiguous future report field presentedTestPattern with explicit offscreen render-target and display-presentation fields, then annotated the historical M3 renderer evidence so it cannot be mistaken for onscreen presentation proof.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Metal render report field names or notes distinguish offscreen render timing from display presentation
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: apple/Sources/MadobeClientCore/MetalRenderTimingProbe.swift now emits renderTargetKind, offscreenTestPatternRendered, and displayPresented; evidence/m4-metal-evidence-wording-polish/render-report.json records renderTargetKind offscreen-texture and displayPresented false.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Existing M3 evidence references are updated or annotated without changing historical raw evidence
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/notes.md and reports/qd/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/{completion,audit}.json annotate historical presentedTestPattern as offscreen validation; evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/render-report.json is unchanged.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Swift tests and macOS check remain green
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-metal-evidence-wording-polish/xcodebuild-metal-probe.log records 2 focused Metal tests passing; evidence/m4-metal-evidence-wording-polish/macos-check.log records just macos-check passing with 16 Xcode tests.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No cross-device presentation claim is added
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-metal-evidence-wording-polish/notes.md records displayPresented false and explicitly excludes live network, received-frame presentation, screenshot evidence, and latency claims.
